# RxDown

> RxDown (formerly RxTaper) is a medication-withdrawal diary app for iOS and
> Android. It helps adults working with a doctor to gradually reduce
> antidepressants, benzodiazepines, sleeping pills, opioids, and other
> long-term prescriptions: AI voice journaling, guideline-based reduction
> schedules (Maudsley, Ashton Manual, Royal College of Psychiatrists),
> receptor-occupancy education, sleep import, and a clinician-ready Doctor
> Report. It is a tracking and education tool, not medical advice.

## Key pages

- [Home](https://rxdown.app/): what RxDown does, feature overview
- [Taper calculator](https://rxdown.app/calculator/): free hyperbolic dose-reduction schedule calculator with methodology and sources
- [Guides](https://rxdown.app/guides/): 12 evidence-based, source-cited education articles — hyperbolic tapering, benzodiazepines (Ashton), DORA and Z-drug sleep medications, brain zaps, withdrawal vs relapse, and more
- [FAQ](https://rxdown.app/faq/): common questions about the app, safety, privacy, and supported medications
- [Privacy](https://rxdown.app/privacy/): local-first data policy — health data stays on the user's device
- [Support](https://rxdown.app/support/): contact

## Full content

- [llms-full.txt](https://rxdown.app/llms-full.txt): the entire English site as clean Markdown, for direct ingestion

## Localized versions

Available in English (/), Traditional Chinese (/zh/), Japanese (/ja/), Korean
(/ko/), German (/de/), Spanish (/es/), French (/fr/), Indonesian (/id/), and
European Portuguese (/pt/).
